back_translation: That same day King Ahasuerus gave the house of Haman, the enemy of the Jews, to Queen Esther. And Mordecai came before the king, because Esther had told what he was to her.
The king removed the signet ring he had recovered from Haman and gave it to Mordecai, and Esther appointed Mordecai over the house of Haman.
Esther spoke to the king once more, falling at his feet, weeping and begging him to avert the evil plan of Haman the Agagite, which he had devised against the Jews.
The king extended the gold scepter toward Esther, and she arose and stood before the king.

She said, 'If it pleases the king, and if I have found favor before him, and if the king approves this matter, and I am pleasing in his sight, let an order be written to revoke the letters that Haman son of Hammedatha the Agagite plotted and wrote to destroy the Jews in all the king's provinces.
For how could I bear to see the disaster that would befall my people, and how could I bear to see the destruction of my kindred?'
King Ahasuerus said to Queen Esther and to Mordecai the Jew, 'Behold, I have given Haman's house to Esther, and he was hanged on the gallows because he plotted to harm the Jews.

Now you yourselves write concerning the Jews as you see fit, in the name of the king, and seal it with the king's signet ring; for a letter written in the king's name and sealed with the king's signet ring cannot be revoked.'

At that time, on the twenty-third day of the third month, the month of Sivan, the royal scribes were summoned, and a decree was written according to all that Mordecai ordered, to the Jews and to the satraps, governors, and princes of the 127 provinces from India to Cush, to each province in its own script, to each people in its own language, and to the Jews in their own script and language.
Mordecai wrote in the name of King Ahasuerus and sealed it with the king's signet ring, and sent the documents by couriers riding swift royal horses, bred from the royal stud.

The letters stated that the king permitted the Jews in every city to assemble and defend their lives, to destroy, kill, and annihilate the forces of any people or province that came to attack them, including their children and women, and to plunder their goods.
On a single day throughout all the provinces of King Ahasuerus, the thirteenth day of the twelfth month, the month of Adar.
A copy of the decree was to be issued as law in every province and published to all peoples, so that the Jews would be ready on that day to avenge themselves on their enemies.
The couriers riding the swift royal horses went out in great haste by the king's command, and the decree was issued in the citadel-city of Susa.
Mordecai went out from the presence of the king wearing royal garments of blue and white, with a large gold crown and a robe of fine purple linen. And the city of Susa shouted for joy.
For the Jews it was a time of light, gladness, joy, and honor.

In every province and every city where the king's command and decree reached, there was joy and gladness among the Jews, with feasting and a holiday. And many of the people of the land became Jews, because the fear of the Jews had fallen upon them.
